issues.title: Page is missing a skip link to main content
issues.description: A skip link allows keyboard and screen reader users to bypass repetitive navigation and jump directly to main content. Without it, users must navigate through all navigation elements on every page. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Add a skip link as the first element in the body, typically styled to be visually hidden until focused: Skip to main content Then ensure your main content has id="main-content".
issues.fixSnippet:
Skip to main content ...
...

issues.title: Page is missing an <h1> element
issues.description: Every page should have exactly one <h1> that describes the main purpose or topic. The <h1> is the primary heading and helps users and assistive technology understand page structure. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion:
Add a descriptive

at the beginning of your main content:

Welcome to My Site

issues.title: Page is missing an <h1> element
issues.description: Every page should have exactly one <h1> that describes the main purpose or topic. The <h1> is the primary heading and helps users and assistive technology understand page structure. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion:
Add a descriptive

at the beginning of your main content:

Welcome to My Site

issues.title: <section> is missing a heading or accessible name
issues.description: Sections should have an associated heading or accessible name so screen reader users can understand the section purpose. Without it, the section is unlabeled and confusing. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion:
Add a heading inside the section or provide aria-label / aria-labelledby:

Section Title

...

issues.title: Interactive control relies only on title for accessible name
issues.description: The title attribute alone is not a reliable accessible name for interactive controls. Screen readers may not announce it consistently. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Provide a visible label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby instead of relying solely on title.

issues.title: Interactive control relies only on title for accessible name
issues.description: The title attribute alone is not a reliable accessible name for interactive controls. Screen readers may not announce it consistently. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Provide a visible label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby instead of relying solely on title.

issues.title: <ul> is empty or has no <li> children
issues.description: Lists should contain at least one list item (<li>). An empty list confuses users and serves no semantic purpose. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion:
Add list items to the list or remove the empty list element:
  • Item 1
  • Item 2

issues.title: <li> is not inside <ul> or <ol>
issues.description: List items must be direct children of <ul> or <ol>. Using <li> outside of a list breaks semantic structure and confuses screen readers. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Move the <li> inside a <ul> (unordered) or <ol> (ordered) list, or convert <li> to a different element if it is not a list item.

issues.suggestion:
Add a descriptive

issues.description: Sections should have an associated heading or accessible name so screen reader users can understand the section purpose. Without it, the section is unlabeled and confusing. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion:
Add a heading inside the section or provide aria-label / aria-labelledby:

Section Title

...

issues.title: <a> has no accessible name
issues.description: Links must have visible text or an accessible name so screen reader users can understand their destination. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Provide visible link text or an aria-label/aria-labelledby value that describes the link target.

issues.title: <a> is missing a valid href attribute
issues.description: An anchor without a valid href is not a real navigational link, and keyboard or screen reader users cannot use it like a normal link. Use a <button> for actions or provide a real URL for navigation.
issues.suggestion: Use a real href for navigation, or replace the anchor with a button if the element performs an action instead of linking to another page.

issues.title: <a> link has no visible purpose
issues.description: Links must have a clear, understandable purpose. This link has no visible text, aria-label, or title. Screen reader users cannot determine where the link goes. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.4 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Provide visible link text or an aria-label that describes the link destination. For icon-only links:

issues.title: <a> has no accessible name
issues.description: Links must have visible text or an accessible name so screen reader users can understand their destination. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Provide visible link text or an aria-label/aria-labelledby value that describes the link target.

issues.title: <a> is missing a valid href attribute
issues.description: An anchor without a valid href is not a real navigational link, and keyboard or screen reader users cannot use it like a normal link. Use a <button> for actions or provide a real URL for navigation.
issues.suggestion: Use a real href for navigation, or replace the anchor with a button if the element performs an action instead of linking to another page.

issues.title: <a> link has no visible purpose
issues.description: Links must have a clear, understandable purpose. This link has no visible text, aria-label, or title. Screen reader users cannot determine where the link goes. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.4 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Provide visible link text or an aria-label that describes the link destination. For icon-only links:

issues.title: aria-label is overriding visible text
issues.description: When both aria-label and visible text are present, aria-label takes precedence for screen readers. If they differ, it creates confusion between what sighted and non-sighted users see. Violates WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 (Level A).
issues.suggestion: Either remove aria-label if visible text is sufficient, or ensure aria-label matches or extends the visible text meaningfully.
